title: unable to resolve container names with netavark/aardvark
body:
Seems we have a use case that is failing:

Run a container:
`$ sudo podman run -dt --rm --dns-search dns.podman --network podman1 --name web --network-alias web1 --network-alias web2 alpine top`

Verify name resolution

```
$ sudo podman run -it --rm --dns-search dns.podman --network podman1 alpine /bin/sh
/ # apk update
fetch https://dl-cdn.alpinelinux.org/alpine/v3.15/main/x86_64/APKINDEX.tar.gz
fetch https://dl-cdn.alpinelinux.org/alpine/v3.15/community/x86_64/APKINDEX.tar.gz
v3.15.0-258-gd5d32e35cf [https://dl-cdn.alpinelinux.org/alpine/v3.15/main]
v3.15.0-268-gaf6454ec89 [https://dl-cdn.alpinelinux.org/alpine/v3.15/community]
OK: 15857 distinct packages available
/ # apk add bind-tools
(1/13) Installing fstrm (0.6.1-r0)
(2/13) Installing krb5-conf (1.0-r2)
(3/13) Installing libcom_err (1.46.4-r0)
(4/13) Installing keyutils-libs (1.6.3-r0)
(5/13) Installing libverto (0.3.2-r0)
(6/13) Installing krb5-libs (1.19.2-r4)
(7/13) Installing json-c (0.15-r1)
(8/13) Installing protobuf-c (1.4.0-r0)
(9/13) Installing libuv (1.42.0-r0)
(10/13) Installing xz-libs (5.2.5-r0)
(11/13) Installing libxml2 (2.9.12-r2)
(12/13) Installing bind-libs (9.16.25-r0)
(13/13) Installing bind-tools (9.16.25-r0)
Executing busybox-1.34.1-r3.trigger
OK: 13 MiB in 27 packages
/ # ip a
1: lo: <LOOPBACK,UP,LOWER_UP> mtu 65536 qdisc noqueue state UNKNOWN qlen 1000
    link/loopback 00:00:00:00:00:00 brd 00:00:00:00:00:00
    inet 127.0.0.1/8 scope host lo
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
    inet6 ::1/128 scope host 
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
2: eth0@if72: <BROADCAST,MULTICAST,UP,LOWER_UP,M-DOWN> mtu 1500 qdisc noqueue state UP qlen 1000
    link/ether b2:b4:0b:e7:37:dd brd ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff
    inet 10.89.1.5/24 brd 10.89.1.255 scope global eth0
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
    inet6 fe80::b0b4:bff:fee7:37dd/64 scope link 
       valid_lft forever preferred_lft forever
/ # dig web

; <<>> DiG 9.16.25 <<>> web
;; global options: +cmd
;; Got answer:
;; ->>HEADER<<- opcode: QUERY, status: NOERROR, id: 12151
;; flags: qr rd ra; QUERY: 1, ANSWER: 0, AUTHORITY: 0, ADDITIONAL: 1

;; OPT PSEUDOSECTION:
; EDNS: version: 0, flags:; udp: 4096
;; QUESTION SECTION:
;web.				IN	A

;; Query time: 0 msec
;; SERVER: 192.168.99.1#53(192.168.99.1)
;; WHEN: Wed Feb 09 13:59:40 UTC 2022
;; MSG SIZE  rcvd: 32

/ # echo $?
0
/ # cat /etc/resolv.conf 
search dns.podman dns.podman
nameserver 10.89.1.1
nameserver 192.168.99.1
nameserver 192.168.99.1
```

It seems like the --search-dns is a factor here.

comments.body:
@baude I think this already resolved here: https://github.com/containers/aardvark-dns/pull/73 and verified here in @lsm5 's PR https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/13144

I'm closing this issue but please re-open if something is unresolved.
